title: Multi-Site Analysis for Independent Data Sources
text: process exploratory analysis cross-sectional longitudinal configurations. First, execute either Single Site, Exploratory analyses, configured appropriately study, data source. median_all_* columns reflect overall median using method. typically used baseline comparison metric dr_output. mind lack overall comparison metric just like compare site-level medians, need union table together adjust output_function accordingly. like overall median available, need output patient-level results addition standard summary output (patient_level_tbl = T). patient-level output returned data source, need union tables together recompute overall median.
code: library(duplicaterecords)  my_table <- dr_process(cohort = my_cohort,                        multi_or_single_site = 'single',                        anomaly_or_exploratory = 'exploratory',                        time = T / F,                        patient_level_tbl = T / F                        ...) my_final_results <- my_table1 %>% dplyr::union(my_table2) ... %>%   dplyr::union(my_table_n) %>%   dplyr::mutate(output_function = 'dr_ms_exp_(cs/la)') my_combined_results <- my_table1 %>% dplyr::union(my_table2) ... %>%   dplyr::union(my_table_n) %>%   dplyr::mutate(output_function = 'dr_ms_exp_(cs/la)')  ## Cross-Sectional my_final_results <- my_combined_results %>%   group_by(duplicate_definition) %>%   summarise(median_all_with0s = as.numeric(median(duplicate_row_pp)),             median_all_without0s = as.numeric(median(duplicate_row_pp[duplicate_row_pp!=0]))) %>%   mutate(median_all_without0s = ifelse(is.na(median_all_without0s), 0L, median_all_without0s))  ## Longitudinal my_final_results <- my_combined_results %>%   group_by(duplicate_definition, time_start, time_end) %>%   summarise(median_all_with0s = as.numeric(median(duplicate_row_pp)),             median_all_without0s = as.numeric(median(duplicate_row_pp[duplicate_row_pp!=0]))) %>%   mutate(median_all_without0s = ifelse(is.na(median_all_without0s), 0L, median_all_without0s))

title: Multi-Site Analysis for Independent Data Sources
text: Multi-site anomaly detection can executed proportional results produced module. , may follow instructions Multi-Site, Exploratory output without producing patient-level results, proceed appropriate anomaly detection method .

title: Multi-Site Analysis for Independent Data Sources
text: producing combined result set, execute code (using functions squba.gen package) run anomaly detection analysis. p_value can selected user.
code: df_start <- compute_dist_anomalies(df_tbl = my_combined_results,                                    grp_vars = c('duplicate_definition'),                                    var_col = {`duplicate_row_prop`, `duplicate_pt_prop`},                                    denom_cols = c('duplicate_definition', 'total_pt',                                                    'total_rows'))  df_final <- detect_outliers(df_tbl = df_start,                             tail_input = 'both',                             p_input = p_value,                             column_analysis = {`duplicate_row_prop`, `duplicate_pt_prop`},                             column_variable = 'duplicate_definition') %>%   mutate(output_function = 'dr_ms_anom_cs')

title: Multi-Site Analysis for Independent Data Sources
text: producing combined result set, execute code (using functions squba.gen package) run anomaly detection analysis.
code: df_final <- ms_anom_euclidean(fot_input_tbl = my_combined_results,                               grp_vars = c('site', 'duplicate_definition'),                               var_col = {`duplicate_row_prop`, `duplicate_pt_prop`}) %>%   mutate(output_function = 'dr_ms_anom_la')

title: Compute duplicate record distributions & per patient medians — compute_dr
text: cohort table cohort members least site, person_id, start_date, end_date dr_tbl table information definitions duplication examined analysis. table contain following columns: definition_alias | character | string identify definition duplication used analysis domain_tbl | character | CDM table evaluated duplicate rows duplicate_columns | character | name field unmapped values identified include_exclude | character | string control whether duplicate_columns included excluded define duplicates concept_field | character | string name field domain table concepts located (codeset provided) date_field | character | name field domain table date used temporal filtering vocabulary_field | character | PCORnet applications, name field domain table vocabulary identifier differentiate concepts one another (ex: dx_type); can set NA OMOP applications codeset_name | character | (optional) name codeset can used define variable interest (ex: evaluate unit completeness specific drug) filter_logic | character | (optional) logic applied domain_tbl order achieve definition interest; written applying dplyr::filter command R site_col name column site information (either site site_summ) grouped_list list columns used group analysis tables time boolean indicating whether analysis executed time omop_or_pcornet string indicating data model underlying CDM data (either omop pcornet)

title: Multi-Site, Exploratory, Cross-Sectional — dr_ms_anom_cs
text: dot plot shape dot represents whether point anomalous, color dot represents proportion unmapped rows/patients given duplicate_definition, size dot represents mean proportion across sites
code: if there were no groups eligible for analysis, a heat map showing the proportion     and a dot plot showing each site's average standard deviation away from the mean     proportion is returned instead

title: Duplicate Records – Output Generation — dr_output
text: Using tabular output generated dr_process, function build graph visualize results. function configuration output bespoke ggplot. Theming can adjusted user graph output using + theme(). graphs can also made interactive using make_interactive_squba()

title: Duplicate Records – Output Generation — dr_output
code: dr_output(   process_output,   output_col,   filter_definition = NULL,   text_wrapping_char = 60L,   large_n = FALSE,   large_n_sites = NULL )

title: Duplicate Records – Output Generation — dr_output
text: process_output tabular input || required tabular output produced dr_process output_col string || defaults NULL name numerical variable process_output used generate plot. input required checks EXCEPT Multi Site, Anomaly Detection, Longitudinal filter_definition string vector || defaults NULL string vector names duplicate definitions focus analysis. single value accepted following checks: Multi Site, Anomaly Detection, Longitudinal Single Site, Anomaly Detection, Longitudinal Multiple values accepted : Multi Site, Exploratory, Cross-Sectional Multi Site, Exploratory, Longitudinal text_wrapping_char integer || defaults 60 integer indicating length limit text wrapping long labels plots. Generally applies position duplicate_definition plot, labels can lengthy large_n boolean || defaults FALSE Multi-Site analyses, boolean indicating whether large N visualization, intended high volume sites, used. visualization produce high level summaries across sites, option add specific site comparators via large_n_sites parameter. large_n_sites vector || defaults NULL large_n = TRUE, vector site names can add site-level information plot comparison across high level summary information.

title: Duplicate Records – Output Generation — dr_output
text: function produce graph visualize results dr_process based parameters provided. default output typically static ggplot gt object, interactive elements can activated passing plot make_interactive_squba. detailed description output specific check type, see PEDSpace metadata repository

title: Duplicate Records – Output Generation — dr_output
Duplicate Records — dr_process
text: completeness module assess distribution unmapped values, proportionally examining median number unmapped values per patient. user provide variables (dr_input_file) evaluated define values considered "unmapped" (NULL considered default). Sample versions inputs, OMOP PCORnet, included data package accessible duplicaterecords::. Results can optionally stratified site, age group, /time. function compatible OMOP PCORnet CDMs based user's selection.

title: Duplicate Records — dr_process
code: dr_process(   cohort,   dr_input_file,   omop_or_pcornet,   patient_level_tbl = FALSE,   multi_or_single_site = "single",   anomaly_or_exploratory = "exploratory",   time = FALSE,   time_span = c("2012-01-01", "2020-01-01"),   time_period = "year",   p_value = 0.9,   n_sd = 2,   age_groups = NULL,   output_level = c("row", "patient") )

title: Duplicate Records — dr_process
text: cohort tabular input || required cohort used data quality testing. table contain, minimum: site | character | name(s) institutions included cohort person_id / patid | integer / character | patient identifier start_date | date | start cohort period end_date | date | end cohort period Note start end dates included table used limit search window analyses module. dr_input_file tabular input || required table information duplicate record definitions examined analysis. table contain following columns: definition_alias | character | string identify definition duplication used analysis domain_tbl | character | CDM table evaluated duplicate rows duplicate_columns | character | names columns define duplication, either including excluding include_exclude | character | string control whether duplicate_columns included excluded define duplicates concept_field | character | string name field domain table concepts located (codeset provided) date_field | character | name field domain table date used temporal filtering vocabulary_field | character | PCORnet applications, name field domain table vocabulary identifier differentiate concepts one another (ex: dx_type); can set NA OMOP applications codeset_name | character | (optional) name codeset can used define variable interest (ex: evaluate unit completeness specific drug) filter_logic | character | (optional) logic applied domain_tbl order achieve definition interest; written applying dplyr::filter command R see example structure file, please see ?duplicaterecords::dr_input_file_omop ?duplicaterecords::dr_input_file_pcornet omop_or_pcornet string || required string, either omop pcornet, indicating CDM format data patient_level_tbl boolean || defaults FALSE boolean indicating whether additional table patient level results output. TRUE, output function list containing summary patient level output. Otherwise, function just output summary dataframe multi_or_single_site string || defaults single string, either single multi, indicating whether single-site multi-site analysis executed anomaly_or_exploratory string || defaults exploratory string, either anomaly exploratory, indicating type results produced. Exploratory analyses give high level summary data examine fact representation within cohort. Anomaly detection analyses specialized identify outliers within cohort. time boolean || defaults FALSE boolean indicate whether execute longitudinal analysis time_span vector - length 2 || defaults c('2012-01-01', '2020-01-01') vector indicating lower upper bounds time series longitudinal analyses time_period string || defaults year string indicating distance dates within specified time_span. Defaults year, time periods month week also acceptable p_value numeric || defaults 0.9 p value used threshold Multi-Site, Anomaly Detection, Cross-Sectional analysis n_sd numeric || defaults 2 Single Site, Anomaly Detection, Cross-Sectional analysis, number standard deviations used threshold identify outlier age_groups tabular input || defaults NULL like stratify results age group, create table CSV file following columns use input parameter: min_age | integer | minimum age group (.e. 10) max_age | integer | maximum age group (.e. 20) group | character | string label group (.e. 10-20, Young Adult, etc.) like stratify age group, leave NULL output_level string || defaults row string indicating analysis level use basis Multi Site, Anomaly Detection computations Acceptable values either patient row
